news.t: Best Low-Volatility ETFs for When the Market is a Roller Coaster
5 ETFs to Protect Against the Market’s Wild Swings

holdings.infoBox: EFAV is an equity ETF with a total of 289 individual holdings. The top holdings are Iberdrola stock at 1.58%, SECOM CO., LTD. at 1.51%, Novartis AG at 1.44%, Shell at 1.40%, and Eni S.p.A. at 1.39%.

overview.description: The iShares MSCI EAFE Min Vol Factor ETF (EFAV) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the MSCI EAFE Minimum Volatility index. The fund tracks an index of developed-market equities, ex-US and Canada, selected and weighted to create a low volatility portfolio subject to constraints including sector exposure. EFAV was launched on Oct 18, 2011 and is issued by BlackRock.

data.title: Best Low-Volatility ETFs for When the Market is a Roller Coaster
data.text: It's been a volatile few years for the stock market, and that has investors sniffing out low-volatility ETFs. Just understand their strengths and their limitations.

data.title: 5 ETFs to Protect Against the Market’s Wild Swings
data.text: ETFs invested in defensive companies with a history of raising dividends offer investors a hedge in volatile markets.

data.title: ETF Edge, May 13, 2019
data.text: Bob Pisani breaks down the latest ETF trends with Tom Lydon of ETFTrends.com and Alfred Eskandar of Salt Financial.
